settings.title: Built for mobile conversion
settings.copy: Sticky ATC, quick media scanning, and clear checkout actions keep the page app-like instead of cluttered.

settings.title: Transparent support
settings.copy: Shipping, returns, and support cues stay close to the buy action so shoppers do not need to hunt for trust.

settings.title: Single focused upsell
settings.copy: The pairing block is intentionally restrained so it lifts AOV without creating decision overload.

settings.heading: Shipping and returns
settings.body: Use concise shipping expectations and a clear damaged-or-defective support policy so checkout feels lower risk.

settings.heading: Materials or specs
settings.body: Surface the practical details that matter to buying confidence instead of dumping a giant spec sheet at the top of the page.

settings.heading: Who this is best for
settings.body: Use a fast answer here so paid-social traffic can self-qualify without scrolling through too much copy.

settings.heading: Social-first proof around the buy flow

settings.author: @launchcreator
settings.title: Show the product doing one obvious job really well
settings.caption: Use short-form style captions and vertical media crops so the section feels shareable and native to paid-social traffic.

settings.author: @realcustomer
settings.title: Use-case media beats generic review stars
settings.caption: Keep the proof visual and practical: before-and-after, reaction, routine fit, or context shot.

settings.author: @typsh.it
settings.title: Design this like a mini swipeable reel row
settings.caption: It should feel closer to content discovery than a legacy testimonial carousel.

settings.question: What problem does this solve?
settings.answer: Answer in the same direct language used in paid creative so the product page feels consistent with the ad promise.

settings.question: How fast is shipping?
settings.answer: Clarify timing, tracking, and what support should do if something looks delayed.

settings.question: What if the item arrives damaged?
settings.answer: Keep the support route plain, visible, and reassuring without pushing shoppers into a separate policy maze.

settings.question: Who is this best for?
settings.answer: Use this slot to help the shopper decide fast whether the product fits their routine or use case.
